findings.message: Layering violation: create_item opens its own SessionLocal() and performs the ORM write inline, bypassing both the get_session request-scoped dependency and the existing repository.create_item() seam. The sibling get_items route uses SessionDep (Depends(get_session)) and delegates to list_items(); this route breaks that established routes -> repository boundary by importing SessionLocal directly from db.engine (line 7) and talking to the database itself.
findings.suggestion: Inject the session via the SessionDep dependency and delegate the write to the repository, e.g. `def create_item(payload: ItemCreate, session: SessionDep) -> Item: return repository.create_item(session, payload.name)`. Drop the `SessionLocal` import. Note repository.create_item already commits; the inline version omits session.commit() before refresh, so the row would never be persisted.
